title: Inflatable bladder and system including same
abstract: An inflatable bladder for supporting a posterior portion of a person while seated on a seat. The bladder having a general downward directed U-shape having a top head portion and two leg portions that extend down from the head portion. The head portion is arranged to be generally rear to the lumbar vertebrae and the leg portions are arranged to generally extend down on both sides of the pelvis region, by preferably sloping down and further preferably wrapping around the pelvis region.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. An inflatable bladder having a general downward directed U-shape comprising a top head portion and two leg portions that extend down from the head portion, wherein the bladder is configured to be mounted to posterior and/or lateral portions of a person such that when the person is seated on a seat the head portion is arranged to be generally rear to the lumbar vertebrae and the leg portions are arranged to generally extend down on both sides of the pelvis region, by preferably sloping down and further preferably wrapping around the pelvis region. 2. The inflatable bladder of claim 1, wherein the bladder's U-shape being configured to permit the pelvis region, preferably the sacral section, to be adjacent a backrest of the seat, preferably at least partially abutting the backrest. 3. The inflatable bladder of claim 2, wherein the bladder's head portion being arranged to be aligned with the person's rib-cage, possibly a lower area of the rib-cage. 4. The inflatable bladder of claim 3, wherein the leg portions being configured to slant downwards away from the backrest. 5. The inflatable bladder of claim 4 and comprising means for securing the bladder to the seat, wherein the means possibly comprises a strap. 6. The inflatable bladder of claim 5 and comprising a fixture for at least partially supporting the bladder relative to the seat. 7. The inflatable bladder of claim 6 wherein at least part of the fixture being arranged to support location of at least part of the leg portions relative to the seat. 8. The inflatable bladder of claim 7, wherein at least part of bladder being located between segments of the fixture. 9. The inflatable bladder of claim 8, wherein at least two of the segments are pivotally coupled. 10. The inflatable bladder of claim 3 and being divided into sub divisions. 11. The inflatable bladder of claim 10, wherein the sub divisions are sealed one from the other or at least partially in fluid communication with each other. 12. The inflatable bladder of claim 3, wherein the seat is a car seat in a vehicle environment. 13. The inflatable bladder of claim 3, wherein the seat is a seat of an office, home and/or public environment. 14. A seating system comprising a seat and an inflatable bladder located on the seat, the inflatable bladder being for supporting a posterior portion of a person while seated on a seat, the bladder having a general downward directed U- shape comprising a top head portion and two leg portions that extend down from the head portion, wherein the head portion being arranged to be generally rear to a person's lumbar vertebrae when seated on the seat and the leg portions being arranged to generally extend down on both sides of a person's pelvis region when seated on the seat. 15. The seating system of claim 14, wherein the bladder's U- shape being configured to permit a person's pelvis region when seated on the seat, preferably the sacral section, to be adjacent a backrest of the seat, preferably at least partially abutting the backrest. 16. The seating system of claim 15, wherein the bladder's head portion being arranged to be generally aligned with the person's rib-cage, possibly a lower area of the rib-cage. 17. The seating system of claim 15, wherein the leg portions being configured to slant downwards away from the backrest. 18. The seating system of claim 17 and comprising means for securing the bladder to the seat, wherein the means possibly comprises a strap. 19. The seating system of claim 18 and comprising a fixture for at least partially supporting the bladder relative to the seat. 20. The seating system of claim 19 wherein at least part of the fixture being arranged to support location of at least part of the leg portions relative to the seat. 21. The seating system of claim 19, wherein at least part of the bladder being located between segments of the fixture. 22. The seating system of claim 21, wherein at least two of the segments are pivotally coupled. 23. The seating system of claim 14 and being divided into sub divisions. 24. The seating system of claim 23, wherein the sub divisions are sealed one from the other or at least partially in fluid communication with each other. 25. The seating system of claim 14, wherein the seat is a car seat in a vehicle environment. 26. The seating system of claim 14, wherein the seat is a seat of an office, home and/or public environment. 27. A method for supporting a posterior portion of a person seated on a seat, the method comprising providing an inflatable bladder having a general downward directed U- shape comprising a top head portion and two leg portions that extend down from the head portion, and locating the bladder relative to the person to position the head portion generally rear to the lumbar vertebrae and the leg portions generally along lateral sides of the pelvis region. 28. The method of claim 27 and comprising inflation and deflation of the bladder. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein inflation of the bladder's head portion is configured to urge the lumbar vertebrae to flex forwardly and increase lordosis while the pelvis region generally remains substantially in place. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein inflation of at least part of the bladder is configured to provide lift to the rib cage. 31. The method of claim 30 and being provided with a controller for affecting inflation and/or deflation of the bladder. 32. The method of claim 31 and being provided for supporting the posterior portion of the person in a vehicle. 33. The method of claim 31 wherein inflation and/or deflation of the bladder is according to data communicated to the controller. 34. The method of claim 33 wherein the data comprises information from a navigation system. 35. The method of claim 33 wherein the data defines a pre-defined inflation and/or deflation pattern, preferably a previously saved personalized sequence. 36. The method of claim 33 wherein the data is shared data provided by other people, possibly using a generally similar inflatable bladder. 37. An inflatable bladder having a general downward directed U- shape comprising a top head portion and two leg portions that extend down from the head portion, wherein the bladder is configured for supporting a posterior portion or a person such that when the person is seated the bladder being configured to provide: a forward directed force applied upon the lumbar vertebrae, an upward directed lift force bearing upwards upon the rib-cage, and inward lateral directed forces bearing upon the pelvis region. 38. The inflatable bladder of claim 37, wherein the bladder being configured to bear upwards upon a lower portion of the person's rib cage. 39. The inflatable bladder of claim 38, wherein inflation of the bladder being configured to increase lordosis in the person's vertebral column. 40. The inflatable bladder of claim 39, wherein the increase in lordosis occurs while the person's pelvis region generally remains substantially in place. 41. A method for supporting a posterior portion of a person while seated in a vehicle, the method comprising the steps of: providing an inflatable bladder configured to generally bear against the posterior region of the person, and controlling inflation and/or deflation of the bladder according to navigational data representing a route of travel of the vehicle. 42. The method of claim 41, wherein an inflation level of the bladder changes when the navigational data indicates that vehicle is within or about to enter a route section with high frequency of sharp turns.
